title: Opening-hours schedule examples
description: Canonical example schedules for the six common venue shapes. Used by the SDUI hours-editor screen's 'Start from a template' picker + by the observer's default-seed logic when the caller doesn't supply opening_hours at creation time.

examples.description: Typical office / admin-facility schedule. Weekend closed.
Typical fitness venue. Long daily window, seven-day operation. Slight weekend curtailment.
Badge-access facility open around the clock. Members badge in outside staffed hours.
examples.note: 23:59 stands in for midnight — the evaluator treats end-of-day and next-day-00:00 equivalently.

examples.description: Common in Spain / Italy / parts of the Middle East: two service periods separated by an afternoon closure. Requires branch_multi_period_hours entitlement.

examples.display_label: Academic year (term-time weekdays + school holidays closed)
examples.description: School-attached venue. Weekend / summer / winter breaks are exception blocks that override the base weekday schedule. Requires branch_multi_period_hours + is a template — tenants populate the exception dates on save.
examples.note: The example exceptions are placeholder dates — tenants edit them to their actual calendar.

examples.description: Facility that hosts events only on weekends. Weekdays closed by default; specific tournament dates added as exceptions with hours override.

notes: Every time in every schedule is LOCAL to the Branch's Region timezone. Never UTC. The evaluator converts `now()` to the branch's tz before comparing.

The observer's default seed (used when the caller doesn't supply opening_hours) picks `standard_business_hours` when `branch.hours.default_weekend_closed=true` and `standard_gym_hours` (adjusted to config defaults) otherwise.

`close < open` in a period is refused by `valid_opening_hours`. To model an overnight period (e.g. 22:00-06:00), split it into two periods: `{open: 22:00, close: 23:59}` on day N + `{open: 00:00, close: 06:00}` on day N+1. Requires `branch_multi_period_hours` on both days.
